title: Atlantid Core — Generic Join Contract (C3) — REPLACEMENT CANDIDATE
description: REPLACEMENT_CANDIDATE (awaiting Gate B). Net-new Core schema for artifact C3 (requirement P3-REQ-ADD-005) under operator Ruling 2 (P3-REQ-ADD-012). This is the GENERIC join contract that lives in Atlantid Core: it declares stable feature identifiers, coordinate-reference behavior, horizontal/vertical units, vertical datum, origin, transforms, and version/continuity rules — the single published surface every buyer-side layer joins to. It is GENERIC ONLY. It defines join SEMANTICS and deterministic COMPATIBILITY EXPECTATIONS but contains NO source-specific deterministic join recipe and NO externally derived association table; those belong to the separately licensed external data packs they serve and are held outside Core. Counsel question 9 (ODbL derivative-database status) remains open and is NOT resolved here; no legal conclusion is drawn. Fail-closed.

x-atlantid-operator-ruling.P3-REQ-ADD-012: Core contains the generic join contract only. Source-specific deterministic join recipes ship with the external packs they serve; externally derived join tables remain outside Core; counsel question 9 remains open and is not converted into legal advice.

contract_class.description: Fixed. This artifact is the generic Core join contract, never a source-specific recipe.

stable_identifiers.description: The join key. Stable, identity-neutral, tile-scoped feature identifiers that survive versioning under the continuity rules.

prohibited_identity_implications.description: A feature_id MUST NOT imply any of these. Closed set.

coordinate_reference_behavior.description: How coordinates behave for join. Global truth stays float64; render is tile-local with an explicit global transform.

vertical_datum.description: Declared vertical datum, e.g. the source_vertical_crs realization. Named, never assumed.

transforms.description: Ordered, deterministic transform steps. Every alignment transform must be derived from USGS sources only, never by comparison against OpenStreetMap or any external layer.

generic_join_semantics.description: The generic join rule: Atlantid ships a clean substrate plus this join contract; the buyer brings their own domain layer (parcels, zoning, addresses, roads, scenarios) and joins it themselves through declared coordinates, transforms, and the stable feature_id.

deterministic_compatibility.description: Deterministic compatibility expectations a buyer-side join can rely on.

compatibility_expectation.description: e.g. 'feature_id stable within a (geometry_revision, algorithm_revision) pair; a join computed against version v is valid for that version and re-validated on version bump.'

id_change_conditions.description: feature_id may change only if geometry or algorithm revision changes; on change, package_version bumps and the receipt records prior->new revision.

continuity_rule.description: Identifiers are never mutated in place under a fixed version.

core_boundary.description: Ruling 2 boundary, machine-asserted and fail-closed. Core must not contain any source-specific recipe or externally derived join table.
